publications.title: Structure of TCR and antigen complexes at an immunodominant CTL epitope in HIV-1 infection.
publications.authors: Shimizu A, Kawana-Tachikawa A, Yamagata A, Han C, Zhu D, Sato Y, Nakamura H, Koibuchi T, Carlson J, Martin E, Brumme CJ, Shi Y, Gao GF, Brumme ZL, Fukai S, Iwamoto A.
publications.executive_summary: This study presents crystal structures of HLA-A*2402-bound HIV-1 Nef134-10 peptide complexed with wild-type and escape-mutant TCRs, revealing how the virus evades CD8+ T-cell recognition through sequential mutations at positions 135 and 139. The Y135F mutation, though disrupting a key hydrogen bond to HLA, is functionally compensated by a newly formed interaction at residue 138, allowing the epitope to maintain its overall conformation while becoming poorly recognized by wild-type TCRs; conversely, the late-appearing F139L mutation represents a true escape variant that emerges in patients lacking the Y135F mutation. TCR clones from patients exposed to Y135F or capable of cross-recognizing both variants adopt distinct binding modes to accommodate the mutant peptide, indicating that structural TCR plasticity can partially overcome viral immune escape. These findings illustrate how HIV-1 selects for sequential epitope mutations under CTL pressure and explain the population-level dynamics of escape variant emergence during chronic HIV infection.
